client-tools.cfly.not_in_world: §cCannot toggle flight: not connected to a world.
client-tools.cfly.not_allowed: §cFlight is not allowed here. You need to be in creative mode or a flight-enabled region.
client-tools.cfly.autojump_enabled: §aAuto-jump mode enabled. §7Flight will auto-jump when toggled on.
client-tools.cchat.not_connected: §cCannot send chat: not connected to a server.
client-tools.cbow.help.brief: §6/cbow §7— Arrow trajectory prediction. Use §e/cbow help §7for details.
client-tools.cbow.help.overview: §7Predicts arrow trajectory when holding a bow or crossbow. Shows the parabolic arc and landing point based on charge, enchantments, and player velocity.
client-tools.cbow.help.status: §e/cbow status  §7Show current state and held weapon info.
client-tools.cbow.help.on_detail:
§6Subcommand: on
§7Enables arrow trajectory prediction. While active, aiming with a bow or crossbow will show a predicted parabolic arc and landing marker.
§7Use §e/cbow off §7to disable.
client-tools.cbow.help.off_detail:
§6Subcommand: off
§7Disables arrow trajectory prediction. The trajectory overlay will no longer appear when holding a bow or crossbow.
client-tools.cbow.help.status_detail:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Shows the current state (enabled/disabled), the weapon held, its charge level, and active enchantments.
§7Useful for checking what the prediction system sees.
client-tools.cbow.help.unknown_subcomman: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/cbow help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.cdoll.not_found: §e%s is not in the doll list.
client-tools.cdoll.help.overview: §7Apply the Furina doll 3D model to any item. The doll shows the original item's texture on its card face.
client-tools.cdoll.help.add: §e/cdoll add <item>  §7Register an item for doll model replacement. Tab-completes item IDs.
client-tools.cdoll.help.remove: §e/cdoll remove <item>  §7Unregister an item from doll appearance. Tab-completes from current doll list.
client-tools.cdoll.help.clear: §e/cdoll clear  §7Remove all doll assignments and delete the generated resource pack.
client-tools.cdoll.help.example:
§7Examples:
  §f/cdoll add minecraft:diamond
  §f/cdoll add diamond
  §f/cdoll remove minecraft:diamond
  §f/cdoll list
  §f/cdoll clear
client-tools.cdoll.help.add_detail:
§6Subcommand: add
§7Adds an item to the doll appearance list. A resource pack with the doll model override is automatically generated, enabled, and loaded.
§7The item can be specified as a full ID (§fminecraft:diamond§7) or a bare name (§fdiamond§7).
§7Resource packs are reloaded after adding — this may take a few seconds.
§7Examples:
  §f/cdoll add minecraft:netherite_sword
  §f/cdoll add stick
client-tools.cdoll.help.remove_detail:
§6Subcommand: remove
§7Removes an item from the doll appearance list. The model override file is deleted and resources are reloaded.
§7Tab-completion shows only items currently in the doll list.
§7Examples:
  §f/cdoll remove minecraft:diamond
  §f/cdoll remove stick
client-tools.cdoll.help.list_detail:
§6Subcommand: list
§7Shows all items currently configured with doll appearances.
§7Displays the full item ID for each entry.
client-tools.cdoll.help.clear_detail:
§6Subcommand: clear
§7Removes ALL doll assignments and deletes the generated resource pack. Resources are reloaded.
§7Cannot be undone — you will need to re-add items if you want them back.
client-tools.cdoll.help.unknown_subcomma: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/cdoll help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.ctimer.count_invalid: §cCount must be a positive integer or "infinite".
client-tools.ctimer.count_parse_error: §cInvalid count: %s. Use a positive integer or "infinite".
client-tools.ccraft.count_set_infinite: §aTarget count set to: §einfinite §7(until materials run out or box full)
client-tools.ccraft.count_set_finite: §aTarget count set to: §e%d §7(max final products to craft)
client-tools.ccraft.show_color_invalid: §cInvalid color: %s. Use hex (FF5555) or name (red, green, blue...).
client-tools.ccraft.no_table_found: §cNo crafting table found nearby. Use §e/ccraft station <x> <y> <z>
client-tools.ccraft.source_equals_produc: §eSource and product are the same item.
client-tools.ccraft.output_box_full: §c/ccraft: Output box appears full after §e%d§c attempts. Stopping.
client-tools.ccraft.auto_scanning: §7System will auto-scan the input chest and build an optimal crafting plan.
client-tools.ccraft.auto_no_materials: §cNo materials found in input chest that can craft §e%s§c.
client-tools.ccraft.auto_nothing_to_craf: §eInput chest is empty, waiting for materials...
client-tools.ccraft.station_list_empty: §eNo saved stations. Use §e/ccraft save <name> §eto create one.
client-tools.ccraft.nearest_not_found: §eNo saved stations with valid positions. Set positions first with §e/ccraft station§e.
client-tools.ccraft.save_no_name: §cPlease provide a station name. Usage: §e/ccraft save <name>
client-tools.csweep.blockdetect_mode_set: §aBlockage action set to: §cSTOP & WAIT §7— player will hover until blocks behind are cleared.
§aBlockage action set to: §eSLOW §7— speed drops to minimum when unmined blocks detected.
client-tools.csweep.mode_drain: §aMode set to §bDrain Assist §a(DRAIN). §7Will auto-buy sand from shop when below 1 stack.
client-tools.csweep.mode_running: §cCannot change mode while sweep is running. Stop the sweep first.
client-tools.csweep.show_layer_on: §aLayer emphasis enabled. §7Current Y-level: §athick bright §7| Other Y-levels: §7dimmed §7(uses sweep Y when running, player Y when idle)
client-tools.csweep.show_layer_off: §eLayer emphasis disabled. All layers shown equally.
client-tools.csweep.not_flying: §cYou must be flying to start sweep. Use §e/cfly
client-tools.csweep.crafting_running: §cCannot start sweep while crafting is active.
client-tools.csweep.paused: §eSweep paused at station §e%d§e/§e%d§7. Progress saved. Use §e/csweep start §7or §e/csweep pause §7to resume.
client-tools.csweep.resume_failed: §cFailed to resume: saved state not found or world changed.
client-tools.csweep.reconnect_reminder: §eYou have an unfinished sweep (station §6%d§e). Use §6/csweep start §eto resume, or §6/csweep reset §eto discard.
client-tools.csweep.litematica.sync_on_n: §aLitematica sync enabled, but no area selection is active.
client-tools.csweep.litematica.not_avail: §eLitematica is not installed or not loaded.
client-tools.csweep.nearest_hint: §7Move around to find the best starting point. Run §e/csweep start §7to begin, or §e/csweep nearest §7again to turn off tracking.
client-tools.csweep.nearest_cleared_paus: §ePaused state cleared — tracking nearest station instead.
client-tools.csweep.penalty_floating_war: §e⚠ Floating with no block below — server may apply 5× mining penalty.
client-tools.csweep.penalty_spoof_note: §d→ onGround is being spoofed to true in movement packets.
client-tools.csweep.penalty_water_detail: §b→ Underwater mining penalty — 5× slower. 25× if also floating!
client-tools.csweep.help.penalty_detail:
§6Subcommand: penalty
§7Shows real-time mining penalty diagnostics to help debug slow sweep speeds:
  §7- Flight / onGround state (actual vs spoofed)
  §7- Whether a solid block is directly below you
  §7- Water / cobweb / stuck-in-block detection
  §7- Movement blockage status (server rejecting packets)
  §7- Backward hemisphere blockage count
  §7- Effective speed vs configured speed
§7Use this when your sweep is running unexpectedly slow and you need to identify which penalty is active.
client-tools.csequence.already_running: §cA sequence is already running. Use §e/csequence stop §cfirst.
client-tools.csequence.crafting_running: §cCannot run sequence while crafting is active.
client-tools.csequence.list_empty: §eNo sequences saved. Use §e/csequence new <name> §eto create one.
text.client-tools.config.flight_descript: §7Control flight state through this toggle or use §e/cfly§7.
text.client-tools.config.craft_descripti: §7Configure crafting automation parameters. Same as §e/ccraft§7 subcommands.
tooltip.client-tools.cfly.autojump: §7When enabled, /cfly and /cfly enable will also make the player jump.
tooltip.client-tools.ccraft.source: §7Source item to start from (optional). Leave empty for auto-detect mode.
tooltip.client-tools.ccraft.count: §7How many final products to craft. §e-1 §7= infinite (until materials run out).
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.speed: §7Blocks per second (0.5–100). Minimum speed when auto speed is enabled.
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.autospeed: §7Automatically adjust speed based on block density ahead. Speeds up over empty areas, slows down over dense areas.
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.maxspeed: §7Maximum speed when auto speed is enabled (0.5–100).
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.avoidwater: §7When auto speed is on, raise or lower Y to keep the player out of water, avoiding the underwater mining penalty.
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.blockdetect: §7Scan the hemisphere behind the player for unmined blocks. Excludes liquids and unbreakable blocks (bedrock, etc.). Slows down or stops when blocks are left behind.
tooltip.client-tools.sweep.blockdetect_w: §7ON: Stop and hover in place until blocks behind are cleared. OFF: Slow down to minimum speed when unmined blocks are detected.
text.client-tools.config.litematica_unav: §cLitematica not installed. Install Litematica to enable area sync.
text.client-tools.config.timer_empty: §7No active timers. Use §e/ctimer start §7to create one.
client-tools.ctimer.help.brief: §6/ctimer §7— Timed command scheduler. Use §e/ctimer help §7for details.
client-tools.ctimer.help.overview: §7Runs commands on a repeating timer. Great for automating repetitive tasks like checking coordinates, sending status messages, or running periodic commands.
client-tools.ctimer.help.start:
§e/ctimer start <count> interval <duration> <command>
  §7Start a new timer. §bcount§7: number of times (1,5,10,30, or §dinfinite§7). §bduration§7: interval between runs (e.g. §f1s§7, §f5m§7, §f1h§7, §f500ms§7, §f10t§7). §bcommand§7: the command to run.
client-tools.ctimer.help.stop:
§e/ctimer stop <pattern>
  §7Stop timers whose ID contains §bpattern§7. The pattern matches against the timer's auto-generated ID.
client-tools.ctimer.help.list:
§e/ctimer list
  §7List all active timers with their ID, command, interval, and remaining count.
client-tools.ctimer.help.example: §7Example: §f/ctimer start 5 interval 30s cchat Mining progress update
client-tools.ctimer.help.start_detail:
§6Subcommand: start
§7Creates a new repeating timer.
  §bcount§7: How many times to run. Use §dinfinite§7 for endless.
  §bduration§7: Time between executions (suffix: ms/s/m/h/t).
  §bcommand§7: Any client command (tab-completed from registered commands).
§7Example: §f/ctimer start 10 interval 1m cchat Checking in!
client-tools.ctimer.help.start_example:
§7More examples:
  §f/ctimer start infinite interval 5s cfly status
  §f/ctimer start 3 interval 500ms cchat Hello
client-tools.ctimer.help.stop_detail:
§6Subcommand: stop
§7Stops timers whose ID contains the given pattern.
§7The ID is shown when the timer starts and in §e/ctimer list§7.
§7Example: §f/ctimer stop cchat §7— stops all timers running cchat commands.
client-tools.ctimer.help.stop_all_detail:
§6Subcommand: stop-all
§7Immediately stops every active timer. No confirmation — use with care.
client-tools.ctimer.help.list_detail:
§6Subcommand: list
§7Shows all active timers with details: ID, command, interval, and how many runs remain.
client-tools.ctimer.help.unknown_subcomm: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/ctimer help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.cfly.help.overview: §7Toggles creative-style flight. Works in survival if the server allows it (mayfly=true).
client-tools.cfly.help.root: §e/cfly  §7Toggle flight on/off. This is the default action.
client-tools.cfly.help.jump: §e/cfly jump [enable|disable]  §7Toggle auto-jump mode. When on, enabling flight also makes you jump off the ground.
client-tools.cfly.help.status: §e/cfly status  §7Show current flight state (mayfly, flying, gamemode, autojump).
client-tools.cfly.help.enable_detail:
§6Subcommand: enable
§7Turns flight on. If auto-jump is enabled, also jumps off the ground.
§7Reports if flight is already enabled.
client-tools.cfly.help.disable_detail:
§6Subcommand: disable
§7Turns flight off. Reports if flight is already disabled.
client-tools.cfly.help.jump_detail:
§6Subcommand: jump
§7Toggles auto-jump mode. When ON, §e/cfly§7 and §e/cfly enable §7will also make the player jump.
client-tools.cfly.help.jump_sub:
§7  §e/cfly jump enable  §7— Turn auto-jump on.
§7  §e/cfly jump disable §7— Turn auto-jump off.
client-tools.cfly.help.status_detail:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Displays: mayfly permission, current flying state, gamemode, and auto-jump setting.
client-tools.cfly.help.unknown_subcomman: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/cfly help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.ccraft.help.brief: §6/ccraft §7— Automated crafting chain. Use §e/ccraft help §7for details.
client-tools.ccraft.help.overview: §7Automates multi-step crafting. Set source/product items, station/input/output chest positions, then run. Supports recipe chain analysis and smart multi-source mode.
client-tools.ccraft.help.source:
§e/ccraft source <item>  §7Set the starting/source item. Use §fclear §7to reset.
§7  Tab-completion: item IDs from registry.
client-tools.ccraft.help.product:
§e/ccraft product <item>  §7Set the target product item (required). Use §fclear §7to reset.
§7  Tab-completion: item IDs from registry.
client-tools.ccraft.help.station: §e/ccraft station [x y z]  §7Set crafting table position. Without coords, uses the block you're looking at. Use §fclear §7to reset.
client-tools.ccraft.help.input: §e/ccraft input [x y z]  §7Set input chest position. Without coords, uses the block you're looking at. Use §fclear §7to reset.
client-tools.ccraft.help.output: §e/ccraft output [x y z]  §7Set output chest position. Without coords, uses the block you're looking at. Use §fclear §7to reset.
client-tools.ccraft.help.count: §e/ccraft count <number|infinite>  §7Set how many final products to craft. §dinfinite §7= until materials run out. Use §fclear §7to reset to 1.
client-tools.ccraft.help.show:
§e/ccraft show [duration] [station_color] [input_color] [output_color]
  §7Highlight configured blocks. §bduration§7: e.g. §f3s§7, §f10s§7, §f1m§7. §bcolors§7: name (red, blue...) or hex (FF5555).
client-tools.ccraft.help.status: §e/ccraft status  §7Show current configuration and runtime progress.
client-tools.ccraft.help.run: §e/ccraft run  §7Start the crafting execution. Auto-finds crafting table if station not set. Shows recipe chain or enters smart multi-source mode.
client-tools.ccraft.help.quick_start:
§7Quick start:
  §f/ccraft product minecraft:diamond_block
  §f/ccraft station §7(look at crafting table)
  §f/ccraft input §7(look at input chest)
  §f/ccraft output §7(look at output chest)
  §f/ccraft count 64
  §f/ccraft run
client-tools.ccraft.help.source_detail:
§6Subcommand: source
§7Sets the starting material item. In legacy mode, the system analyzes the recipe chain from source→product.
§7Leave empty (use §f/ccraft source clear§7) to enable smart multi-source auto-detect mode.
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft source minecraft:oak_log
  §f/ccraft source clear
client-tools.ccraft.help.product_detail:
§6Subcommand: product
§7Sets the target item you want to craft (required).
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft product minecraft:diamond_block
  §f/ccraft product clear
client-tools.ccraft.help.station_detail:
§6Subcommand: station
§7Sets the crafting table/workbench position.
§7Aim at the block and type §e/ccraft station§7, or specify coordinates manually.
§7If not set, the system auto-detects nearby crafting tables when you run.
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft station §7(aim at table)
  §f/ccraft station 100 64 200
  §f/ccraft station clear
client-tools.ccraft.help.input_detail:
§6Subcommand: input
§7Sets the input chest position where source materials are taken from.
§7Aim at the chest or specify coordinates.
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft input §7(aim at chest)
  §f/ccraft input 100 64 200
  §f/ccraft input clear
client-tools.ccraft.help.output_detail:
§6Subcommand: output
§7Sets the output chest position where crafted products are deposited.
§7Aim at the chest or specify coordinates.
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft output §7(aim at chest)
  §f/ccraft output 100 64 200
  §f/ccraft output clear
client-tools.ccraft.help.count_detail:
§6Subcommand: count
§7Sets the target number of final products to craft.
§7Use §dinfinite §7to keep crafting until materials run out or output chest is full.
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft count 64
  §f/ccraft count infinite
  §f/ccraft count clear §7(resets to 1)
client-tools.ccraft.help.show_detail:
§6Subcommand: show
§7Highlights configured block positions with colored outlines.
§7Args: duration (e.g. 3s, 10s, 1m), then optionally colors for station/input/output.
§7Colors: names (red, green, blue, gold, yellow, cyan, magenta, white, orange, purple, pink, lime, aqua, navy, teal, brown, gray, black) or hex (FF5555).
§7Examples:
  §f/ccraft show 10s
  §f/ccraft show 30s red green blue
  §f/ccraft show 5s FF5555 55FF55
client-tools.ccraft.help.status_detail:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Shows current configuration (source, product, positions, count) and runtime status (running/done/error, progress, cycle count).
client-tools.ccraft.help.run_detail:
§6Subcommand: run
§7Starts the automated crafting process. If source is set, uses recipe chain mode. If source is not set, uses smart multi-source auto-detect mode (scans input chest for available materials).
§7Also auto-highlights configured blocks for visual confirmation.
client-tools.ccraft.help.stop_detail:
§6Subcommand: stop
§7Stops the running crafting process immediately. Progress is not saved.
client-tools.ccraft.help.clear_detail:
§6Subcommand: clear
§7Resets all /ccraft settings: source, product, station, input, output, and count.
client-tools.ccraft.help.save: §e/ccraft save <name>  §7Save current settings as a named station.
client-tools.ccraft.help.load: §e/ccraft load <name>  §7Load a saved station's settings. Tab-complete available.
client-tools.ccraft.help.list: §e/ccraft list  §7List all saved stations with positions, products, and distances.
client-tools.ccraft.help.delete: §e/ccraft delete <name>  §7Delete a saved station. Tab-complete available.
client-tools.ccraft.help.nearest: §e/ccraft nearest  §7Auto-select and load the station closest to your current position.
client-tools.ccraft.help.save_detail:
§6Subcommand: save
§7Saves all current /ccraft settings (product, source, station, input, output, count) as a named station profile.
§7If the name already exists, it will be overwritten.
§7Example: §f/ccraft save home_base
client-tools.ccraft.help.load_detail:
§6Subcommand: load
§7Loads a previously saved station profile, restoring all its settings.
§7Tab-completion shows all saved station names.
§7Example: §f/ccraft load home_base
client-tools.ccraft.help.list_detail:
§6Subcommand: list
§7Lists all saved station profiles with their positions, target product, and distance from you.
§7The active station is marked with §e▶§7.
client-tools.ccraft.help.delete_detail:
§6Subcommand: delete
§7Permanently deletes a saved station profile.
§7Tab-completion shows all saved station names.
§7Example: §f/ccraft delete old_outpost
client-tools.ccraft.help.nearest_detail:
§6Subcommand: nearest
§7Finds the saved station closest to your current position and loads it automatically.
§7Useful for quickly switching to the crafting setup at your current location.
§7Requires at least one saved station with a valid station position.
client-tools.ccraft.help.unknown_subcomm: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/ccraft help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.csweep.help.brief: §6/csweep §7— Snake-pattern area sweep. Use §e/csweep help §7for details.
client-tools.csweep.help.overview: §7Automated snake-pattern area traversal for mining/carpet-bombing. Moves the player through a defined cuboid in a sweeping pattern so other mods can handle the actual block breaking.
client-tools.csweep.help.pos:
§e/csweep pos1 [x y z]  §7Set first corner of the cuboid. §e/csweep pos2 [x y z]  §7Set second corner.
§7  Without coords, uses the block you're looking at (or your position).
client-tools.csweep.help.radius: §e/csweep radius <1..64>  §7Set station spacing in blocks. Larger = fewer stations, sparser coverage. Typing without value shows current radius.
client-tools.csweep.help.speed: §e/csweep speed <0.5..100>  §7Set flight speed in blocks/sec. Typing without value shows current speed.
client-tools.csweep.help.maxspeed: §e/csweep maxspeed <0.5..100>  §7Set max speed for auto-speed mode. Typing without value shows current.
client-tools.csweep.help.autospeed: §e/csweep autospeed [on|off]  §7Toggle adaptive speed based on block density ahead. Faster over air, slower through dense areas.
client-tools.csweep.help.avoidwater: §e/csweep avoidwater [on|off]  §7Toggle Y-level adjustment to stay out of water (avoids underwater mining speed penalty).
client-tools.csweep.help.blockdetect:
§e/csweep blockdetect [on|off|wait|slow]  §7Toggle backward hemisphere blockage detection.
  §fwait§7: stop and hover until blocks behind are cleared. §fslow§7: drop to min speed.
client-tools.csweep.help.mode:
§e/csweep mode [mining|drain]  §7Switch sweep mode.
  §fmining§7: Mining assist (default) — movement-only sweep.
  §fdrain§7: Drain assist — auto-detects low sand and buys from shop.
client-tools.csweep.help.show:
§e/csweep show  §7Show display settings.
  §fshow outline [on|off]§7 — toggle cuboid outline
  §fshow path [on|off]§7 — toggle path lines
  §fshow layer [on|off]§7 — toggle layer emphasis
  §fshow dir [on|off]§7 — toggle nearest station direction line
client-tools.csweep.help.litematica: §e/csweep litematica [on|off|sync]  §7Configure Litematica area sync. Automatically uses Litematica's selection as the sweep area.
client-tools.csweep.help.nearest: §e/csweep nearest [on|off]  §7Toggle real-time nearest station tracking. Shows the closest unswept station and updates as you move.
client-tools.csweep.help.expand_contract:
§e/csweep expand <blocks>  §7Expand cuboid in the direction you're facing.
§e/csweep contract <blocks>  §7Contract cuboid in the direction you're facing.
client-tools.csweep.help.next: §e/csweep next  §7Skip to the next Litematica sub-region (only when using Litematica sync).
client-tools.csweep.help.control:
§e/csweep start  §7Begin sweeping.
§e/csweep stop   §7Halt the sweep.
§e/csweep pause  §7Toggle pause/resume.
§e/csweep status §7Show config + progress.
§e/csweep penalty §7Show mining penalty diagnostics.
§e/csweep reset  §7Clear all settings.
client-tools.csweep.help.penalty: §e/csweep penalty  §7Show mining penalty diagnostics (water, floating, onGround spoof, etc.).
client-tools.csweep.help.quick_start:
§7Quick start:
  §f/cfly §7(enable flight first!)
  §f/csweep pos1 §7(look at first corner)
  §f/csweep pos2 §7(look at second corner)
  §f/csweep radius 8
  §f/csweep speed 10
  §f/csweep start
client-tools.csweep.help.pos_detail:
§6Subcommand: pos1 / pos2
§7Defines the two corners of the sweep cuboid.
§7Look at the desired corner block and run without coords, or specify x y z manually.
§7Setting a position auto-enables the outline highlight.
§7Examples:
  §f/csweep pos1 §7(aim at block)
  §f/csweep pos2 100 64 -50
client-tools.csweep.help.radius_detail:
§6Subcommand: radius
§7Sets the spacing between sweep stations (1-64 blocks).
§7Smaller radius = denser coverage, more stations, longer sweep time.
§7Typing §e/csweep radius §7without a value shows the current setting.
client-tools.csweep.help.speed_detail:
§6Subcommand: speed
§7Sets the base flight speed in blocks per second (0.5-100).
§7When auto-speed is OFF, this is the constant speed used.
§7When auto-speed is ON, this is the minimum speed.
§7Typing without value shows current speed.
client-tools.csweep.help.maxspeed_detail:
§6Subcommand: maxspeed
§7Sets the maximum speed for auto-speed mode (0.5-100).
§7When auto-speed is ON, the system varies speed between §espeed §7and §emaxspeed §7based on block density.
§7Typing without value shows current max speed.
client-tools.csweep.help.autospeed_detai:
§6Subcommand: autospeed
§7Toggles adaptive speed mode. When ON, speed automatically adjusts:
§7  §aFast §7over empty areas (up to maxspeed)
§7  §eSlow §7through dense blocks (down to speed/minimum)
§7Toggle with no argument, or use §fon§7/§foff §7explicitly.
client-tools.csweep.help.avoidwater_deta:
§6Subcommand: avoidwater
§7Toggles water avoidance. When ON and auto-speed is active, the system adjusts Y-level to keep the player out of water, avoiding the underwater mining speed penalty.
§7Toggle with no argument, or use §fon§7/§foff §7explicitly.
client-tools.csweep.help.blockdetect_det:
§6Subcommand: blockdetect
§7Toggles backward hemisphere blockage detection. Scans blocks behind the player for unmined blocks (excluding liquids and unbreakable blocks like bedrock).
§7Modes:
  §fon§7/§foff §7— enable/disable detection
  §fwait §7— stop and hover until blocks behind are cleared
  §fslow §7— drop to minimum speed when unmined blocks detected
client-tools.csweep.help.mode_detail:
§6Subcommand: mode
§7Switch the sweep operation mode.
  §fmining §7— Mining assist (default): movement-only sweep, pair with other mining mods.
  §fdrain §7— Drain assist: same as mining but monitors sand inventory. When sand drops below 1 stack (64), auto-pauses and buys sand via §e/cbuy sand all §7from the shop, then resumes.
client-tools.csweep.help.show_detail:
§6Subcommand: show
§7Controls visual overlay rendering:
  §foutline [on|off]§7 — cuboid boundary box
  §fpath [on|off]§7 — planned path lines
  §flayer [on|off]§7 — emphasize current Y-layer
  §fdir [on|off]§7 — direction line to nearest station
§7Each toggle can be used without argument to flip, or with §fon§7/§foff§7.
client-tools.csweep.help.litematica_deta:
§6Subcommand: litematica
§7Syncs the sweep area with a Litematica schematic selection.
  §fon§7 — enable sync (auto-detects sub-regions)
  §foff§7 — disable sync (use manual pos1/pos2)
  §fsync§7 — force re-sync from current Litematica selection
§7Requires Litematica mod to be installed.
client-tools.csweep.help.nearest_detail:
§6Subcommand: nearest
§7Toggles real-time nearest station tracking. The system continuously finds the closest unswept station and shows its direction.
§7Useful for finding the optimal starting point before running §e/csweep start§7.
client-tools.csweep.help.expand_contract:
§6Subcommand: expand / contract
§7Adjusts the cuboid boundary in the direction you are currently facing.
§7§eexpand §7increases the size, §econtract §7decreases it.
§7The adjustment axis is auto-detected from your look direction (X/Y/Z).
§7Examples:
  §f/csweep expand 5  §7(looking up = raise ceiling by 5)
  §f/csweep contract 3 §7(looking north = shrink north side by 3)
client-tools.csweep.help.next_detail:
§6Subcommand: next
§7Skips to the next Litematica sub-region. Only available when Litematica sync is enabled and a sweep is running or paused.
client-tools.csweep.help.start_detail:
§6Subcommand: start
§7Begins the sweep operation. Requirements: pos1 and pos2 must be set, player must be flying. The system will auto-enable outline and path highlights.
§7If nearest tracking is active, starts from the nearest station.
client-tools.csweep.help.stop_detail:
§6Subcommand: stop
§7Halts the sweep operation immediately. Progress is saved (can resume with §e/csweep start§7). Use §e/csweep reset §7to discard saved progress.
client-tools.csweep.help.pause_detail:
§6Subcommand: pause
§7Toggles pause/resume. Pausing saves progress so you can resume later. Use again (or §e/csweep start§7) to resume.
client-tools.csweep.help.status_detail:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Shows complete sweep configuration and runtime status: sync source, region count, pos1/pos2, radius, speed, maxspeed, auto-speed, block detect settings, display toggles, volume, and progress bar with ETA.
client-tools.csweep.help.reset_detail:
§6Subcommand: reset
§7Clears all sweep settings and stops any running sweep. Use to start fresh.
client-tools.csweep.help.unknown_subcomm: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/csweep help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.csequence.help.overview: §7Creates, edits, and executes .mcfunction sequence files. Sequences are text files containing one Minecraft command per line, executed in order with configurable delay between each command.
client-tools.csequence.help.new: §e/csequence new <name>  §7Create a new empty .mcfunction file and show its path.
client-tools.csequence.help.edit: §e/csequence edit <name>  §7Show the path of an existing sequence file. Creates it if it doesn't exist. Tab-completes saved names.
client-tools.csequence.help.run:
§e/csequence run <name> [delay] [loop]
  §7Execute a sequence. §bdelay§7: time between commands (default §f1t§7). §bloop§7: keyword §floop §7makes it repeat forever.
  §7Tab-completion: names, common durations, and §floop§7.
client-tools.csequence.help.list: §e/csequence list  §7List all saved sequences with command count and file size.
client-tools.csequence.help.status: §e/csequence status  §7Show current execution progress (IDLE/RUNNING/DONE/STOPPED/ERROR, call stack depth).
client-tools.csequence.help.folder: §e/csequence folder  §7Show the sequences folder path (clickable to copy to clipboard).
client-tools.csequence.help.example:
§7Example:
  §f/csequence new my_sequence
  §7→ Edit the file at the shown path, add commands
  §f/csequence run my_sequence 5t loop
client-tools.csequence.help.new_detail:
§6Subcommand: new
§7Creates an empty .mcfunction file in the sequences folder.
§7The file path is shown as a clickable chat message — click to copy, then open in your text editor.
§7Add one Minecraft command per line (without the / prefix).
§7Example: §f/csequence new farm_routine
client-tools.csequence.help.edit_detail:
§6Subcommand: edit
§7Shows the file path of an existing sequence. Creates the file if it doesn't exist yet.
§7Use this to find the file location before editing.
§7Tab-completion suggests all saved sequence names.
§7Example: §f/csequence edit farm_routine
client-tools.csequence.help.run_detail:
§6Subcommand: run
§7Executes a sequence file, sending each command with the specified delay between them.
  §bname§7: the sequence to run (tab-completed from saved names)
  §bdelay§7: optional, time between commands (default §f1t§7 = 1 tick = 50ms)
  §bloop§7: optional, keyword §floop §7to repeat the sequence endlessly
§7Examples:
  §f/csequence run my_seq
  §f/csequence run my_seq 200ms
  §f/csequence run my_seq 2s loop
client-tools.csequence.help.stop_detail:
§6Subcommand: stop
§7Stops the currently running sequence immediately. Shows which command it stopped at.
client-tools.csequence.help.list_detail:
§6Subcommand: list
§7Lists all saved sequence files with their command count and file size.
client-tools.csequence.help.delete_detai:
§6Subcommand: delete
§7Permanently deletes a sequence file. Cannot be undone.
§7Example: §f/csequence delete old_routine
client-tools.csequence.help.status_detai:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Shows the current state of the sequence executor:
  §7IDLE — no sequence running
  §7RUNNING — actively executing (shows name, progress, delay, next command)
  §7DONE — completed successfully
  §7STOPPED — manually stopped
  §7ERROR — encountered an error
client-tools.csequence.help.folder_detai:
§6Subcommand: folder
§7Shows the path to the sequences folder. The path is clickable — click to copy to clipboard.
client-tools.csequence.help.unknown_subc: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/csequence help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.cplacement.help.brief: §6/cplacement §7— Move Litematica placement along axes. Use §e/cplacement help §7for details.
client-tools.cplacement.help.overview: §7Moves the current Litematica schematic placement/selection along individual X, Y, or Z axes. Useful for fine-tuning placement positions without using Litematica's GUI.
client-tools.cplacement.help.x: §e/cplacement x <amount>  §7Move along the X axis. Positive = east, negative = west.
client-tools.cplacement.help.y: §e/cplacement y <amount>  §7Move along the Y axis. Positive = up, negative = down.
client-tools.cplacement.help.z: §e/cplacement z <amount>  §7Move along the Z axis. Positive = south, negative = north.
client-tools.cplacement.help.status: §e/cplacement status  §7Show the current Litematica selection details (sub-regions, coordinates).
client-tools.cplacement.help.example:
§7Examples:
  §f/cplacement x 5   §7(move 5 blocks east)
  §f/cplacement y -3  §7(move 3 blocks down)
  §f/cplacement z 10  §7(move 10 blocks south)
client-tools.cplacement.help.x_detail:
§6Subcommand: x
§7Moves all sub-regions of the current Litematica selection along the X axis by the given amount.
  §fPositive §7= east (increasing X)
  §fNegative §7= west (decreasing X)
§7Examples:
  §f/cplacement x 5  §7— move 5 blocks east
  §f/cplacement x -10  §7— move 10 blocks west
client-tools.cplacement.help.y_detail:
§6Subcommand: y
§7Moves all sub-regions of the current Litematica selection along the Y axis by the given amount.
  §fPositive §7= up (increasing Y)
  §fNegative §7= down (decreasing Y)
§7Examples:
  §f/cplacement y 3  §7— move 3 blocks up
  §f/cplacement y -5  §7— move 5 blocks down
client-tools.cplacement.help.z_detail:
§6Subcommand: z
§7Moves all sub-regions of the current Litematica selection along the Z axis by the given amount.
  §fPositive §7= south (increasing Z)
  §fNegative §7= north (decreasing Z)
§7Examples:
  §f/cplacement z 7  §7— move 7 blocks south
  §f/cplacement z -4  §7— move 4 blocks north
client-tools.cplacement.help.status_deta:
§6Subcommand: status
§7Displays the current Litematica selection status:
  §7- Whether Litematica is loaded and a selection is active
  §7- Number of sub-regions
  §7- Each sub-region's name and coordinate range
§7Use this to verify the current placement position before moving.
client-tools.cplacement.help.unknown_sub: §cUnknown subcommand: %s. Use §e/cplacement help §cto see all subcommands.
client-tools.cplacement.litematica_not_a: §eLitematica is not installed or not loaded. Please install Litematica to use this command.
client-tools.cplacement.invalid_axis: §cInvalid axis: %s. Use x, y, or z.
client-tools.cplacement.no_selection: §eNo Litematica selection is active. Create a selection first with Litematica's area selection tool.
client-tools.shop.unknown_item: §cUnknown item: %s. Use item ID (e.g. minecraft:sand) or common name (e.g. sand).
client-tools.shop.crafting_running: §cCannot use shop while crafting is active.
client-tools.shop.cache_rebuilding: §eRebuilding shop cache... going to page 1 and scanning all pages.
client-tools.csweep.antiground_on: §aAnti-penalty floor enabled. §7The sweep will shift Y down 0.5 to stand on blocks below stations, using §e%s§7.
client-tools.csweep.antiground_running: §cCannot change anti-penalty floor while sweep is running. Stop the sweep first.
client-tools.csweep.antiground_blacklist: §d⚑ §7IMPORTANT: Add §e%s §7to your printer/miner blacklist to prevent the floor path from being mined!
client-tools.csweep.antiground_no_item: §c⚠ Anti-penalty floor enabled but no §e%s §cfound in inventory. Floor blocks cannot be placed.
client-tools.csweep.help.antiground:
§e/csweep antiground [on|off]  §7Toggle anti-penalty floor path. Places a strip of blocks below stations so the player always stands on solid ground, eliminating the floating mining penalty.
  §f/csweep antiground block <id> §7— set the floor block type (default: minecraft:redstone_ore)
client-tools.csweep.help.antiground_deta:
§6Subcommand: antiground
§7Toggles the anti-penalty floor path feature. When enabled, the sweep:
  §7- Shifts player Y down 0.5 so feet stand on the block below each station
  §7- Attempts to place floor blocks below stations
  §7- Auto-cleans floor blocks by right-clicking after passing
§7Subcommands:
  §fon§7/§foff §7— enable/disable
  §fblock <registry_id> §7— choose the floor block (default: §f§nminecraft:redstone_ore§r§7)
§7Default block: §f§nminecraft:redstone_ore §r§7— right-click update causes it to glow/update.
§7Other good choices: §fobserver§7 (auto-detect on place), §fstone§7 (cheap)
§7§d⚠ Add this block to your printer's blacklist so it doesn't get mined during the sweep.
